name: Alibaba Cloud
description: Alibaba Cloud (Aliyun) is the largest cloud provider in Asia Pacific and fourth globally. Founded in 2009, it provides 200+ services across 28+ regions with particular strength in the Asia Pacific market. It serves as the technology backbone for Alibaba Group's commerce platforms and has extensive experience with massive-scale events like Singles' Day (11.11).

securityModel.name: Alibaba Cloud Shared Responsibility Model
securityModel.description: Alibaba Cloud is responsible for the security of the cloud infrastructure including physical security, network, hypervisor, and the platform layer. Customers are responsible for security configurations, data protection, identity management, and application-layer security within their cloud environment.

layers.scope: Physical datacenter security, network infrastructure, hypervisor security, platform services security, DDoS basic protection
Account security, RAM policies, data encryption and classification, network configuration (security groups, VPC), OS patching, application security

frameworks.name: CIS Alibaba Cloud Foundation Benchmark
frameworks.description: Consensus-based security configuration guidelines for Alibaba Cloud accounts. Covers identity and access management, logging, monitoring, networking, and Virtual Machines. Published by the Center for Internet Security.

frameworks.name: Multi-Level Protection Scheme (MLPS) 2.0
frameworks.description: China's mandatory cybersecurity standard (GB/T 22239-2019) that classifies information systems into five protection levels. Alibaba Cloud provides MLPS-compliant infrastructure for levels 1-4. Relevant for organizations operating in or serving users in China.

frameworks.name: Alibaba Cloud Security Best Practices
frameworks.description: Alibaba Cloud-authored security recommendations covering account management, network isolation, data protection, logging, and incident response. Provides prescriptive guidance for common workload patterns on Alibaba Cloud.

malaysiaContext.notes: Alibaba Cloud has a dedicated Malaysia region (ap-southeast-3) in Kuala Lumpur, making it one of the few hyperscale cloud providers with a local Malaysian region. This is significant for PDPA 2010 data residency requirements. Additional nearby regions include Singapore (ap-southeast-1) and Jakarta (ap-southeast-5). The Malaysia region supports core services including ECS, OSS, RDS, VPC, SLB, and security services.
